meta.description: Weekly cyber threat-intelligence magazine. Aggregates real cyber news + incident feeds, enriches with the live CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, analyses with the provider-abstracted AI (Claude/Ollama), and renders a branded magazine PDF (clean cover + hero feature band + sections + KEV table) which it archives and emails. Every external step has a failure path.

nodes.name: Fetch Energy Sources
nodes.notes: Runs once per feed URL. Non-RSS or unreachable sources are skipped, not fatal.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.OLLAMA_DEFAULT_MODEL || 'llama3.1:8b' }}",
  "stream": false,
  "keep_alive": "10m",
  "options": { "temperature": 0.2, "num_predict": 7500, "num_ctx": 12288 },
  "messages": [
    {"role":"system","content":"You are a senior cyber threat-intelligence analyst at a top consultancy, producing a weekly cyber intelligence magazine for executives. Use ONLY facts found in the provided digests and the live CISA KEV list; never invent organisations, threat actors, CVEs, numbers or quotes. Write in a precise, engaging, magazine-quality editorial voice. Output a SINGLE valid JSON object and nothing else. Keys: weekOf (string); intro (a rich editorial overview of 2-3 paragraphs, about 8-12 sentences total; separate paragraphs with a single newline character; ONE paragraph MUST summarise the overall threat level and the week's dominant trends); weekOverWeek (1-2 sentences on the most important changes since the previous edition, empty string if none); threat (an object with keys: summary (1-2 sentence overview of the threat landscape this week), metrics (array of up to 4 items with keys name, value, change, note - you may leave this empty as live metrics are injected), outlook (2-3 sentences of forward-looking threat predictions), spotlightActor (2-3 sentences profiling the single most significant threat actor or campaign this week), spotlightVuln (2-3 sentences on the single most critical vulnerability this week)); featured (array of the 3-5 most important stories as magazine write-ups, each with keys headline, story which is 3-4 sentences, source which is the article URL or outlet name, and image which is the IMG= URL from the digest if present else an empty string); incidents (array of major incidents and breaches, each with keys org (the affected organisation), development (what happened), theme (sector and business impact), signals (the threat actor or attack vector), risk (MUST begin with Critical, High, Medium or Low then a short rationale), watch (response and what to watch next)); actors (array of active threat actors or campaigns, each with keys name, development (recent activity), theme (origin and type such as nation-state, ransomware or hacktivist), signals (primary targets or sectors), risk (Critical/High/Medium/Low then rationale), watch (TTPs to watch)); vulns (array of notable vulnerabilities each with keys cve, product, vuln, ransomware, date, action - you may leave this empty as live CISA KEV data is injected); featuredIntro, incidentsIntro, actorsIntro, vulnsIntro (each a 1-2 sentence summary paragraph introducing that section); bonus (an object with keys title and body of 3 short paragraphs). Include at least 4 incidents and 4 actors when the digests support it; attribute developments only to entities named in the digests. Keep every text field concise (2-3 sentences maximum) so the COMPLETE JSON is always returned without truncation. Do NOT wrap the output in markdown code fences; your response MUST begin with { and end with }. Output ONLY the JSON object."},
    {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('CYBER NEWS DIGEST for ' + ($json.date||'') + ' (' + ($json.count||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.articles||'') + '

INCIDENTS & RANSOMWARE DIGEST (' + ($json.moversCount||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.moversDigest||'None retrieved.') + '

PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY:
' + ($json.previous||'No previous edition available.') + '

LIVE ACTIVELY-EXPLOITED VULNERABILITIES (CISA KEV - use these for the vulnerabilities table and reference the most critical ones in your analysis):
' + ($json.threatDigest||'Not available this run.')) }} }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Generate Cover (Image Provider)
nodes.notes: Optional cover via Image Provider Abstraction. Dead-end side branch: failure (e.g. no API key) never affects the brief.

nodes.name: Write Report + Archive
nodes.notes: Writes via fs (NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN=fs) to avoid the Read/Write File node's binary requirement.

nodes.name: Reduced Coverage Notice
nodes.notes: Failure path: emit a reduced-coverage notice + log instead of producing nothing.

nodes.name: AI Analyst (Claude)

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "{{ $env.CLAUDE_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}",
  "max_tokens": 16000,
  "system": "You are a senior cyber threat-intelligence analyst at a top consultancy, producing a weekly cyber intelligence magazine for executives. Use ONLY facts found in the provided digests and the live CISA KEV list; never invent organisations, threat actors, CVEs, numbers or quotes. Write in a precise, engaging, magazine-quality editorial voice. Output a SINGLE valid JSON object and nothing else. Keys: weekOf (string); intro (a rich editorial overview of 2-3 paragraphs, about 8-12 sentences total; separate paragraphs with a single newline character; ONE paragraph MUST summarise the overall threat level and the week's dominant trends); weekOverWeek (1-2 sentences on the most important changes since the previous edition, empty string if none); threat (an object with keys: summary (1-2 sentence overview of the threat landscape this week), metrics (array of up to 4 items with keys name, value, change, note - you may leave this empty as live metrics are injected), outlook (2-3 sentences of forward-looking threat predictions), spotlightActor (2-3 sentences profiling the single most significant threat actor or campaign this week), spotlightVuln (2-3 sentences on the single most critical vulnerability this week)); featured (array of the 3-5 most important stories as magazine write-ups, each with keys headline, story which is 3-4 sentences, source which is the article URL or outlet name, and image which is the IMG= URL from the digest if present else an empty string); incidents (array of major incidents and breaches, each with keys org (the affected organisation), development (what happened), theme (sector and business impact), signals (the threat actor or attack vector), risk (MUST begin with Critical, High, Medium or Low then a short rationale), watch (response and what to watch next)); actors (array of active threat actors or campaigns, each with keys name, development (recent activity), theme (origin and type such as nation-state, ransomware or hacktivist), signals (primary targets or sectors), risk (Critical/High/Medium/Low then rationale), watch (TTPs to watch)); vulns (array of notable vulnerabilities each with keys cve, product, vuln, ransomware, date, action - you may leave this empty as live CISA KEV data is injected); featuredIntro, incidentsIntro, actorsIntro, vulnsIntro (each a 1-2 sentence summary paragraph introducing that section); bonus (an object with keys title and body of 3 short paragraphs). Include at least 4 incidents and 4 actors when the digests support it; attribute developments only to entities named in the digests. Keep every text field concise (2-3 sentences maximum) so the COMPLETE JSON is always returned without truncation. Do NOT wrap the output in markdown code fences; your response MUST begin with { and end with }. Output ONLY the JSON object.",
  "messages": [
    {"role":"user","content": {{ JSON.stringify('CYBER NEWS DIGEST for ' + ($json.date||'') + ' (' + ($json.count||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.articles||'') + '

INCIDENTS & RANSOMWARE DIGEST (' + ($json.moversCount||0) + ' items):
' + ($json.moversDigest||'None retrieved.') + '

PREVIOUS EDITION SUMMARY:
' + ($json.previous||'No previous edition available.') + '

LIVE ACTIVELY-EXPLOITED VULNERABILITIES (CISA KEV - use these for the vulnerabilities table and reference the most critical ones in your analysis):
' + ($json.threatDigest||'Not available this run.')) }} }
  ]
}

nodes.notes: Anthropic Claude path (AI_PROVIDER=claude). Higher-quality analysis. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

nodes.name: Build Cover File
nodes.notes: Cover page is its own PDF: full-bleed, no header/footer, no page number.

nodes.name: Gotenberg Cover
nodes.notes: Cover HTML -> single full-bleed PDF page (margins 0, no header/footer).

nodes.name: Stash Cover
nodes.notes: Carry the cover PDF as base64 in JSON so it survives the body conversion HTTP call.

nodes.name: Build Body Files
nodes.notes: Body PDF inputs: content + Gotenberg-recognised header.html (running hero banner) + footer.html (page number).

nodes.name: Gotenberg Body
nodes.notes: Body HTML -> PDF with running hero banner header on every page + page-number footer.

nodes.name: Assemble PDFs
nodes.notes: Place cover + body PDFs on one item with order-prefixed filenames for the merge.

nodes.name: Gotenberg Merge
nodes.notes: Merge clean cover (page 1) in front of the banner+numbered body (pages 2+) into the final brief.

nodes.name: Write PDF
nodes.notes: Saves the branded PDF (binary). Permissions on reports/ allow the container uid to write.

nodes.name: Fetch Live Threat Data
nodes.notes: Live actively-exploited vulnerabilities from the CISA KEV catalog (public, no API key). Non-fatal on failure.

nodes.name: Send to Telegram
nodes.notes: Delivers the finished PDF to Telegram via the Bot API (sendDocument). Uses TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + TELEGRAM_BRIEF_CHAT_ID (falls back to the first TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS entry).
